items.title: Debugger, sanitizers and final project: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? Passing all sanitizer tests certifies that a C++ program has no lifetime or undefined-behavior defects.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Sanitizer results are path-limited evidence; combine multiple appropriate tools with static diagnostics, ownership design, invariants, and boundary tests.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Sanitizer results are path-limited evidence; combine multiple appropriate tools with static diagnostics, ownership design, invariants, and boundary tests.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Sanitizer results are path-limited evidence; combine multiple appropriate tools with static diagnostics, ownership design, invariants, and boundary tests.
Passing all sanitizer tests certifies that a C++ program has no lifetime or undefined-behavior defects.

answer.explanation: Sanitizer results are path-limited evidence; combine multiple appropriate tools with static diagnostics, ownership design, invariants, and boundary tests.
